metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether any Claude-Code-specific phrasing besides 'CLAUDE.md'/'CLAUDE.local.md' actually exists in the skill bodies before listing it as cleanup work; 'the Task tool' currently has zero occurrences in `examples/claude-skills/` and should be removed from the de-risking and AC3 proof command if it was never present.
evidence: Plan text: 'Generalize Claude-Code-specific orchestration phrasing to provider-neutral wording ("subagent" is fine; replace "the Task tool" and similar)'
Plan AC3: `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0
Grep result: search_files('the Task tool', 'examples/claude-skills') → (no matches)
Grep result: search_files('Task tool', 'examples/claude-skills') → (no matches)
findings.finding: The plan's de-risking section asserts 'the Task tool' is Claude-Code-specific phrasing present in the skills that needs to be replaced with provider-neutral wording, but a grep of `examples/claude-skills/` returns zero matches for 'the Task tool' or 'Task tool'. The phrase does not exist in the codebase, making the cleanup work for it a no-op and the premise a false assertion.
findings.location: De-risking results section: 'drop "the Task tool", etc.' and AC3: `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0
scenarios: Implementer searches for 'the Task tool' to replace it, finds nothing, and wastes time wondering if the sweep is broken or the plan is wrong.
AC3's proof command passes trivially for the 'the Task tool' half without any actual de-branding work, providing false assurance.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove 'the Task tool' from the AC3 proof command and the de-risking narrative, or broaden the grep to confirm any such phrasing genuinely exists first (e.g., `rg -l 'Task tool|Claude Code' examples/claude-skills/`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `name: rebar-brainstorm` field to `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md` frontmatter before or as part of the de-branding pass, so all four skills have the complete `name`/`description` schema the plan defines as the portable core.
evidence: Plan text: 'Keep each SKILL.md's name/description frontmatter (the portable core).'
Tool-verified: rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md frontmatter contains only 'description:' — no 'name:' field (lines 1-3).
By contrast, rebar-debug, rebar-implement, and rebar-janitor all carry 'name:' in their frontmatter.
findings.finding: rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md is missing the 'name:' frontmatter field, yet the plan's 'What' section says 'Keep each SKILL.md's name/description frontmatter (the portable core)' — implying all four skills already have it. The plan will silently leave brainstorm with an incomplete frontmatter schema after de-branding, violating the portability claim it is trying to establish.
scenarios: A harness that discovers skills by reading the 'name:' key from SKILL.md frontmatter will not surface rebar-brainstorm, defeating the portability goal.
A reviewer checking AC2 ('no Claude Code-only framing; states SKILL.md portability') will miss this structural incompleteness.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `name: rebar-brainstorm` as the first frontmatter field in `rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md`, matching the pattern in the other three skills.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC3's proving command to also assert zero remaining bare `CLAUDE.md` references in skill bodies, e.g. `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md|CLAUDE\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0 (or a narrower pattern scoped to non-frontmatter lines if `CLAUDE.md` appears legitimately in prose comparisons).
evidence: AC3 grep: `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0
De-risking section: 'Skill-body CLAUDE.md references to repoint to AGENTS.md: rebar-implement/SKILL.md and rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md'
CLAUDE.md (without .local.) is absent from the AC3 grep pattern
findings.finding: AC3's proving command checks for `CLAUDE\.local\.md` but NOT for `CLAUDE\.md` references — yet the plan explicitly states that `rebar-implement/SKILL.md` and `rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md` contain `CLAUDE.md` references that must be repointed to `AGENTS.md`. An executor following AC3 literally could leave bare `CLAUDE.md` references in skill bodies and the AC would still pass green.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3; De-risking results section
scenarios: Executor renames the directory and removes 'CLAUDE.local.md' references but leaves bare 'CLAUDE.md' references in skill bodies; AC3 passes but the de-branding is incomplete.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace AC3 grep with:
`rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md|\bCLAUDE\.md\b' examples/agent-skills/` == 0
(Use `\b` word-boundary to avoid false-positives like `CLAUDE.md` appearing in a 'vs AGENTS.md' comparison sentence, or add a negative-lookahead for 'vs'.)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC3's verify command to also catch bare `CLAUDE.md` references, e.g. `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0 (or add a separate proof line for `rg -c 'CLAUDE\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0).
evidence: AC3 verify: `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0
De-risking: 'Skill-body CLAUDE.md references to repoint to AGENTS.md: rebar-implement/SKILL.md and rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md'
What: 'Repoint internal references from CLAUDE.md/CLAUDE.local.md to AGENTS.md'
findings.finding: AC3's verify command (`rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md'`) does not check for bare `CLAUDE.md` references, yet the plan body and De-risking section explicitly describe repointing `CLAUDE.md` → `AGENTS.md` in `rebar-implement/SKILL.md` and `rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md` as required deliverable work. The criterion's proof step would pass (exit 0) even if those `CLAUDE.md` references remained unchanged.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3; De-risking results section
scenarios: Implementer runs the AC3 verify command after editing only phrasing and CLAUDE.local.md; bare CLAUDE.md references survive undetected; skill bodies still point to the Claude-specific file.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC3 verify command with: `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md|CLAUDE\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0, or split into two lines to distinguish phrasing from file-reference checks.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC3 proving command to also cover bare `CLAUDE.md` references (e.g. `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local?\.md' examples/agent-skills/`) so that both `CLAUDE.md` and `CLAUDE.local.md` repoints are verified.
evidence: AC3 proving command: `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0
De-risking: 'Skill-body CLAUDE.md references to repoint to AGENTS.md: rebar-implement/SKILL.md and rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md'
rebar-implement/SKILL.md:65: `CLAUDE.md` / `CLAUDE.local.md` / `CONTRIBUTING.md` / `docs/`
rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md:10: Read the repo's agent guidance (`CLAUDE.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`)
findings.finding: AC3's proving command (`rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md' examples/agent-skills/`) does not cover bare `CLAUDE.md` references. The plan body and De-risking section explicitly state that `rebar-implement/SKILL.md:65` and `rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md:10` must be repointed from `CLAUDE.md` to `AGENTS.md`, but the AC3 verify command would pass even if those bare `CLAUDE.md` references were never updated, since `CLAUDE.md` (without `.local.`) is not in the regex.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 proving command; De-risking section ('Skill-body CLAUDE.md references to repoint to AGENTS.md')
scenarios: An implementer runs the AC3 verify command after making changes; the command returns 0 (passes) even though CLAUDE.md references in rebar-implement/SKILL.md:65 and rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md:10 were never updated to AGENTS.md — the work item silently escapes the gate.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC3 proving command to: `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md|CLAUDE\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0  (or use `rg -c 'CLAUDE\.local?\.md'` as a single regex covering both forms).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC3 proving command to also verify that bare `CLAUDE.md` references in skill bodies have been repointed (e.g. add `CLAUDE\.md` to the `rg` pattern, or add a separate check), matching the plan's own stated intent in the De-risking section.
evidence: AC3 verify: `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0
De-risking: 'Skill-body CLAUDE.md references to repoint to AGENTS.md: rebar-implement/SKILL.md and rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md'
rebar-implement/SKILL.md:65: `CLAUDE.md` / `CLAUDE.local.md` / `CONTRIBUTING.md` / `docs/`
rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md:10: Read the repo's agent guidance (`CLAUDE.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`)
findings.finding: AC3's proving command (`rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md' examples/agent-skills/`) does not cover bare `CLAUDE.md` references, yet the plan body explicitly directs repointing `CLAUDE.md` → `AGENTS.md` in both rebar-implement/SKILL.md (line 65) and rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md (line 10). An implementer who satisfies all four ACs as written can leave those bare `CLAUDE.md` references untouched and still pass the gate — silently producing skill bodies that reference CLAUDE.md rather than the portable AGENTS.md target the plan requires.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 (proving command) and De-risking results section (CLAUDE.md references to repoint)
scenarios: Implementer replaces only CLAUDE.local.md phrasing and 'the Task tool', runs AC3 prove command, gets zero hits, marks AC3 done — but bare CLAUDE.md references remain unchanged in both skill files.
Reviewers running only the stated prove command also miss the gap.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC3 prove command with: `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md|CLAUDE\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0 (or scope it to non-README files to avoid false positives from historical mentions in prose).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC3 verify command to also check for unrepointed bare `CLAUDE.md` references: e.g. `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local?\.md' examples/agent-skills/` (or add `CLAUDE\.md` as a separate pattern), so the gate catches both forms named in the de-risking section.
evidence: AC3 verify: `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0.
Plan de-risking: 'Skill-body CLAUDE.md references to repoint to AGENTS.md: rebar-implement/SKILL.md and rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md.'
Tool-verified: rebar-implement/SKILL.md:65 — '`CLAUDE.md` / `CLAUDE.local.md` / `CONTRIBUTING.md` / `docs/`'
Tool-verified: rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md:10 — 'Read the repo's agent guidance (`CLAUDE.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`)'
The AC3 pattern matches CLAUDE.local.md but not the bare CLAUDE.md token that appears in both files.
findings.finding: AC3's verification command (`rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md' examples/agent-skills/`) does not match bare `CLAUDE.md` references, yet the plan body and de-risking section explicitly name `CLAUDE.md` as a target to repoint to `AGENTS.md`. Both `rebar-implement/SKILL.md:65` and `rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md:10` contain live `CLAUDE.md` references — these are the exact artifacts the plan says to update, but the AC's own proof command will pass even if they are left unrepointed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section, AC3 / De-risking section / examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md:65 / examples/claude-skills/rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md:10
scenarios: An implementer completes the rename, updates CLAUDE.local.md references, runs the AC3 verify command, gets zero hits, and declares the task done — but both CLAUDE.md references remain, leaving rebar-implement and rebar-janitor pointing at the old file name.
A harness running rebar-implement on a repo that only has AGENTS.md (no CLAUDE.md) will follow the discovery step and find nothing, silently skipping the project-rule discovery phase.
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC3 verify to: `rg -c 'the Task tool|CLAUDE\.local\.md|CLAUDE\.md' examples/agent-skills/` == 0. Note: non-exclusive mentions of CLAUDE.md (e.g. explaining the mapping) may need a count-threshold or a more targeted grep rather than a strict zero.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either specify the canonical per-harness skill-loading paths in the plan (so the executor has a source of truth) or state an explicit default ('document only the harnesses whose paths are already known/confirmed; leave others as TBD with a link') and add an AC that asserts the named harnesses appear in the README.
evidence: 'note which harnesses read skills and from where (paths differ per tool)' — no paths are specified in the plan
AC2 proving command only counts 'Claude Code' occurrences; it does not assert that harness paths are present or correct
Harnesses named in Why: Codex, Copilot, Cursor, Gemini CLI, JetBrains Junie — but their skill-loading paths are not provided
findings.finding: The README update is required to 'note which harnesses read skills and from where (paths differ per tool)' but neither the plan nor the ACs specify which harness paths to document or provide a command to verify their correctness. The executor must independently research or invent the per-harness paths, and AC2's proving command (`rg -c 'Claude Code'`) does not verify that the harness-path content is accurate or present.
findings.location: What section ('note which harnesses read skills and from where'); AC2
scenarios: Executor writes placeholder text like 'each harness has its own path — consult its docs' rather than concrete paths; AC2 passes because 'Claude Code' count is reduced, but the stated goal of multi-harness documentation is not met.
Executor researches paths and gets some wrong; no AC catches this.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a table to the plan listing known harness paths (e.g. Claude Code: `~/.claude/skills/`, Cursor: TBD, etc.) and add an AC like: `rg -c 'agent-skills' examples/agent-skills/README.md` alongside a reviewer confirmation that each listed harness has a concrete path or an explicit 'unknown' marker — rather than leaving the research entirely to the executor.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before drafting the per-harness skill-discovery path table in the README, resolve and cite the authoritative doc for each named harness (Codex, Copilot, Cursor, Gemini CLI, JetBrains Junie) that specifies where it reads skills from.
evidence: Plan text (What): 'note which harnesses read skills and from where (paths differ per tool)'
Plan text (AC2 proving): 'reviewer confirms; rg -c "Claude Code" examples/agent-skills/README.md reduced to only accurate, non-exclusive mentions'
ABSENCE: no per-harness skill-discovery path table or source citations appear anywhere in the plan
ABSENCE: no repo documentation or linked external spec defines these per-tool paths
findings.finding: AC2 directs the README to 'note which harnesses read skills and from where (paths differ per tool),' but the plan provides no verified list of per-harness skill-discovery paths. Publishing unverified per-tool path claims in the README is an unresolved capability assertion about how external tools (Codex, Copilot, Cursor, Gemini CLI, JetBrains Junie) discover SKILL.md files.
findings.location: AC2 — 'states SKILL.md portability and where each harness looks for skills'; What section — 'note which harnesses read skills and from where (paths differ per tool)'
scenarios: An implementer writes per-harness path guidance from memory or inference; the shipped README contains wrong paths for one or more harnesses, directing users to wrong directories.
The reviewer AC2 check ('reviewer confirms') cannot verify per-tool path accuracy without a reference source, so the check is hollow.

evidence: vague/subjective terms present: etc.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Ground the SKILL.md cross-provider adoption claim with at least one verifiable public source (official announcement, spec URL, or per-tool doc link) before the README asserts it as an open standard.
evidence: Plan text: 'the SKILL.md format, which is now a cross-provider OPEN standard (Anthropic's Agent Skills, adopted by Codex, Copilot, Cursor, Gemini CLI, JetBrains Junie, and more)'
ABSENCE: no citation, public spec URL, or official announcement link is provided anywhere in the plan
ABSENCE: no corroborating reference found in any repo file via search for 'Codex Copilot Cursor Gemini CLI JetBrains'
findings.finding: The plan's central justification asserts that SKILL.md is 'a cross-provider OPEN standard (Anthropic's Agent Skills, adopted by Codex, Copilot, Cursor, Gemini CLI, JetBrains Junie, and more)' but provides no citation, spec URL, or verifiable evidence. No corroborating source exists in the repo. If this adoption claim is overstated or incorrect, the README produced under this plan will embed a false portability promise.
findings.location: Why section — 'SKILL.md format, which is now a cross-provider OPEN standard (Anthropic's Agent Skills, adopted by Codex, Copilot, Cursor, Gemini CLI, JetBrains Junie, and more)'
scenarios: The README is written asserting cross-provider portability; a user copies a skill into Codex or Cursor and finds SKILL.md front-matter is not recognized, because the standard is Anthropic-only or the adoption claim was premature.
A future maintainer cites the README's cross-provider claim as authoritative; a reviewer or downstream consumer flags it as marketing fiction.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a footnote or parenthetical in the README that links to the canonical public spec or announcement (e.g. the Anthropic Agent Skills public docs page) and, for each named harness (Codex, Copilot, Cursor, Gemini CLI, JetBrains Junie), links to or quotes the relevant per-tool discovery documentation — or, if those links cannot be found, soften the claim to 'emerging cross-provider convention' with only the harnesses that can be confirmed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default resolution for the AGENTS.md dependency: either (a) name the prerequisite ticket/branch and require it to be merged before this plan runs, or (b) specify that if AGENTS.md is absent the executor should create a minimal stub, or (c) confirm AGENTS.md already exists in the target branch.
evidence: 'Depends on: the AGENTS.md story' — no ticket ID, branch name, or completion check given
No fallback instruction if AGENTS.md is absent at execution time
findings.finding: The plan depends on a sibling 'AGENTS.md story' for the `CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md` repoint to target a real file, but no dependency edge, ticket reference, or fallback is specified. If AGENTS.md does not exist when this plan executes, the executor has no stated guidance on whether to create it, forward-reference it, or block.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Depends on: the AGENTS.md story (so the CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md reference repoint targets the real new file)'
scenarios: Executor runs the plan while the AGENTS.md story is still open; they must decide independently whether to create AGENTS.md, stub it, or skip the repoint — none of these options are authorized by the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback note to the De-risking section (e.g., 'Back-out: `git mv examples/agent-skills examples/claude-skills` and revert the test/doc edits') and confirm whether a redirect or deprecation notice at the old path is warranted for external consumers.
evidence: De-risking: 'Renaming examples/claude-skills → examples/agent-skills is low-risk and CI-safe'
AC1 proof: rg sweep confirms no internal stale references — but external consumers (docs sites, blog posts, other repos) are not addressed
T4 criterion: 'The remedy is an explicit rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract — acknowledgement alone is insufficient'
findings.finding: The plan explicitly renames a public directory path (`examples/claude-skills/` → `examples/agent-skills/`) — a breaking change for any external consumer holding a direct link or copy-paste command to the old path — but states no rollback/back-out plan beyond the implicit git revert, which T4 requires to be explicit.
scenarios: A downstream repo or published tutorial hardlinks to `examples/claude-skills/` and breaks silently; team needs to revert but no plan exists; or the AGENTS.md dependency is unresolved at merge time and the rename ships pointing to a non-existent file.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sequencing guard: either (a) add an AC requiring `AGENTS.md` to exist at the repo root before merging (e.g., `test -f AGENTS.md`), or (b) specify a temporary fallback (e.g., 'reference `AGENTS.md` with a note that it is forthcoming') so readers are not stranded if this lands before the AGENTS.md story.
evidence: 'Depends on: the AGENTS.md story'
Skill-body files rebar-implement/SKILL.md and rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md are listed as needing CLAUDE.md→AGENTS.md repoints
No AC or merge-order guard is specified to ensure AGENTS.md exists before this change lands
findings.finding: The plan explicitly depends on 'the AGENTS.md story' for the CLAUDE.md→AGENTS.md repoint in skill bodies, but provides no sequencing guard — if this change merges before AGENTS.md exists, skill bodies will reference a non-existent file, stranding readers.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Depends on: the AGENTS.md story (so the CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md reference repoint targets the real new file).'
scenarios: This change merges first; a developer following the skill's AGENTS.md reference finds a 404/missing file
A CI doc-link checker flags the broken internal reference
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] `AGENTS.md` exists at repo root (dependency landed); `test -f AGENTS.md` passes.' Alternatively, if sequencing cannot be guaranteed, allow skill bodies to note `AGENTS.md (see AGENTS.md story)` with a comment until the file exists.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit sequencing guard so the `CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md` repoint in skill bodies cannot land until AGENTS.md exists; either (a) add an AC prove command that asserts `AGENTS.md` is present, (b) split the repoint into a separate task that depends on the AGENTS.md story ticket, or (c) if merging before AGENTS.md is acceptable, use a placeholder that is neutrally worded rather than a hard AGENTS.md filename reference.
evidence: Scope: 'Depends on: the AGENTS.md story (so the CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md reference repoint targets the real new file)'
No AC or prove command checks that AGENTS.md exists before or after the change
AGENTS.md is absent from the repo root (confirmed: directory listing shows only CLAUDE.md)
findings.finding: The plan directs skill bodies to reference `AGENTS.md` instead of `CLAUDE.md`, but states 'Depends on: the AGENTS.md story (so the CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md reference repoint targets the real new file)' with no sequencing gate. There is no AC, dependency link, or merge guard that prevents this change from landing before AGENTS.md exists in the repo. If it lands first, agents following the updated skills will be told to read a file that does not exist — which is worse than the pre-rename state.
findings.location: Scope section — 'Depends on:' clause; AC3 (no ordering guard stated)
scenarios: This task is implemented and merged before the AGENTS.md story lands; skill bodies now instruct agents to read a non-existent AGENTS.md, causing silent failures in any harness that follows the instruction.
The dependency is tracked informally and forgotten; CI has no gate to catch it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or a note in AC3) that verifies `AGENTS.md` exists (or that the dependency ticket has landed) before the `CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md` repoint is merged, so skill bodies never reference a file that is absent in the target repo.
evidence: Plan text: 'Depends on: the AGENTS.md story (so the CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md reference repoint targets the real new file).'
No AC or fallback specifies what happens if this plan ships before the AGENTS.md story lands.
rebar-implement/SKILL.md:65 and rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md:10 — the repointed instructions will direct agents to read AGENTS.md, which may not exist.
findings.finding: The plan's 'Scope' and 'What' sections require repointing `CLAUDE.md` → `AGENTS.md` in skill bodies, and the 'Scope' section notes a dependency on 'the AGENTS.md story.' No fallback or conditional is specified for the case where `AGENTS.md` does not yet exist when this rename lands — the skills being updated will instruct agents to read a file that may be absent, producing a silent discovery miss rather than a caught configuration error.
findings.location: Scope section ('Depends on: the AGENTS.md story') / What section (CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md repoint)
scenarios: This plan merges before the AGENTS.md story. Agents running rebar-implement or rebar-janitor follow the 'read AGENTS.md' instruction, find no file, and silently skip project-rule discovery — operating on stale defaults.
A reviewer checking ACs does not notice the missing dependency file because no AC verifies AGENTS.md existence.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
